prerequisites: A running Apache Kafka cluster (version 1.0 or above) that Airbyte can reach over the network.
Network access from Airbyte to the Kafka brokers listed in the bootstrap_servers config field.
Write permissions to the destination topic, and the topic created in advance (or auto.create.topics.enable turned on at the broker, which is not recommended for production).
The broker addresses to populate bootstrap_servers (host:port, comma separated for multiple brokers).
A topic_pattern value, either a static topic name or a dynamic pattern using the {namespace} and {stream} variables.
If the brokers require authentication, SASL credentials for the chosen mechanism (PLAIN, SCRAM-SHA-256, SCRAM-SHA-512, GSSAPI, or OAUTHBEARER) to supply via the protocol.sasl_jaas_config secret.

authMethods.name: SASL (via sasl.jaas.config)
authMethods.summary: Set the protocol config field to SASL_PLAINTEXT or SASL_SSL and provide the secret protocol.sasl_jaas_config. This maps to Kafka's sasl.jaas.config property, which lets a client specify its JAAS login configuration (mechanism plus credentials) inline without a separate config file. Use SASL_SSL to encrypt the authentication exchange over TLS; SASL_PLAINTEXT sends it unencrypted. Supported mechanisms include PLAIN, SCRAM-SHA-256, SCRAM-SHA-512, GSSAPI (Kerberos), and OAUTHBEARER.

authMethods.name: PLAINTEXT (no authentication)
authMethods.summary: Set the protocol config field to PLAINTEXT for unauthenticated, unencrypted connections to brokers that do not require SASL. In this mode the protocol.sasl_jaas_config secret is not used.

setupSteps.title: Prepare the Kafka cluster and topic
setupSteps.body: Ensure a Kafka cluster of version 1.0 or above is running and reachable from Airbyte. Create the destination topic ahead of time and grant write permissions, or enable auto.create.topics.enable on the brokers (not recommended for production).

setupSteps.title: Configure broker connection and routing
setupSteps.body: Set bootstrap_servers to the broker address list (host:port, comma separated). Set topic_pattern to a fixed topic name or a dynamic pattern using {namespace} and {stream}. Optionally set client_id, test_topic for connection validation, and sync_producer.

setupSteps.title: Choose the security protocol
setupSteps.body: Set the protocol field. Use PLAINTEXT for brokers without authentication, or SASL_PLAINTEXT / SASL_SSL when SASL is required. SASL_SSL runs SASL over TLS so credentials are encrypted in transit; SASL_SSL also requires SSL to be configured on the brokers.

setupSteps.title: Provide SASL credentials when authentication is enabled
setupSteps.body: For a SASL protocol, supply the secret protocol.sasl_jaas_config, which is Kafka's sasl.jaas.config JAAS login string carrying the SASL mechanism and credentials. Select a supported mechanism (PLAIN, SCRAM-SHA-256, SCRAM-SHA-512, GSSAPI, or OAUTHBEARER) that matches the brokers.

setupSteps.title: Tune producer behavior (optional)
setupSteps.body: Adjust the Kafka producer settings exposed as config fields to match your throughput and reliability needs: acks, enable_idempotence, retries, compression_type, batch_size, linger_ms, buffer_memory, max_request_size, max_in_flight_requests_per_connection, the request/delivery/block timeouts, socket buffer sizes, and client_dns_lookup.
